title: CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads
learningObjectives: Explain CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads with advanced interview clarity
Implement CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads in Java with clean design
Avoid high-frequency failure patterns in CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads
Apply this concept to production-oriented scenarios
Communicate decisions with concise technical reasoning

sections.title: Why CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads matters
sections.content:
Async composition and lightweight threads improve scalability when used with clear boundaries.

Day 36 is an inflection point between writing code that "works" and writing code that scales in maintainability and production reliability.

Interviewers at this stage evaluate:
1. Internal understanding (how it works, not only syntax)
2. Trade-off reasoning (why this approach over alternatives)
3. Runtime impact (performance, memory, contention, failure behavior)

This topic should be explained with one minimal example, one realistic failure mode, and one mitigation strategy.

If you can defend your design choices clearly, you will stand out from syntax-only answers.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads
sections.content:
### Day 36: CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads
Async composition and lightweight threads improve scalability when used with clear boundaries.

### Deep understanding goal
You should understand the mechanism, its boundaries, and when to avoid it.

### Practical workflow
1. Implement baseline.
2. Add correctness checks and edge handling.
3. Evaluate runtime cost.
4. Refactor for readability and team adoption.

### Interview quality bar
- Explain what problem it solves.
- Show concise Java example.
- Name one pitfall and one mitigation.
- State complexity/runtime implication.
- Compare with one alternative.

### Production angle
In backend systems, this concept influences throughput, reliability, and debugging speed. A good implementation must support operations, not just local correctness.

### Quick self-check
- Can I explain this in <60 seconds?
- Can I code it from memory?
- Can I describe a failure scenario and fix?

### Satyverse — deeper path (theory → practice)
**JVM tuning starts with metrics:** GC logs, heap dumps, async profiler — guesswork wastes time.

**Generational hypothesis:** most objects die young — tune young gen for allocation rate.

**GC algorithms:** throughput vs latency collectors — choose based on SLA (p99 latency vs batch jobs).

**Off-heap / direct buffers** for NIO — understand when OOME is native memory, not heap.

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: CompletableFuture: async supply + thenApply without blocking yet.

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.description: Combine futures: run two async tasks and join results.

sections.title: Advanced Production Pattern
sections.description: Parallel async steps with CompletableFuture.allOf — fan-out then join (Java 17 friendly).

sections.title: Architecture View - Day 36
sections.description: Visual model for CompletableFuture & Virtual Threads

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Assuming thread safety without explicit synchronization strategy.
Blocking thread pools with long-running I/O operations.
Ignoring cancellation/interruption semantics in async flows.
Choosing lock primitives without contention profiling.
No observability around queue depth, latency, and rejection rates.

sections.title: Exercise - CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads Applied Drill
sections.problem:
Implement a Java solution demonstrating **CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads** in a realistic scenario.

Requirements:
1. Include one baseline approach and one improvement.
2. Handle one edge/failure case.
3. Add short explanation of trade-off.
4. Keep code readable and testable.
5. Show sample output.
hints: Use explicit naming for intent clarity.
Write one failure-case test and expected result.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is CompletableFuture?
conceptual.answer: Non-blocking composition of async tasks with callbacks and combining futures.

followUps.question: Interview follow-up: how does **CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads** show up in **production** debugging or **on-call**?

When unsure, say what you would **measure** or **breakpoint-debug** before optimizing. Separate **compile-time** rules from **runtime** behavior in your explanation.

wrongAnswers: CompletableFuture.get never blocks the calling thread.
Virtual threads replace the need for thread pools entirely.
thenApply runs on the same thread that completed the previous stage always.
join() throws checked InterruptedException like get().

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Phase 4 Focus | Quick Rule | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Concept | CompletableFuture and Virtual Threads | Explain mechanism + boundary | Syntax-only answers |
| Runtime | Performance/memory/concurrency | Mention impact explicitly | Hidden runtime costs |
| Code | Clean composable units | Prefer readability first | Over-chained complexity |
| Failure | Known pitfall + mitigation | Name one concrete case | Hand-wavy debugging |
| Interview | Decision reasoning | Compare alternatives | No trade-off discussion |
